headline: Hearing Screening for Newborns Available at Only 42% of Hospitals
context: Universal newborn hearing screening is not yet available at all birthing facilities despite evidence of effectiveness.

cards.big: Only 42% of hospitals with birthing facilities offer newborn hearing screening in 2025
cards.sub: Late hearing loss detection delays speech development affecting 1,500 children annually

cards.big: Average age of hearing loss detection in Malaysia is 2.8 years versus target of 3 months
cards.sub: 2.5-year diagnostic delay means children miss the critical language acquisition window

cards.big: Universal hearing screening costs RM28 per baby but saves RM180,000 in lifetime special education
cards.sub: Cost-benefit ratio of 1 to 6,400 makes hearing screening one of most cost-effective health investments

cards.big: Philippines achieved 85% newborn hearing screening coverage within 3 years of 2019 mandate
cards.sub: Legislative mandate with equipment procurement plan enabled rapid scale-up

cards.big: A RM28 test that saves RM180,000 and transforms a child's life should not be optional
Should newborn hearing screening be mandatory at every birthing facility in Malaysia
